The Court stated that in January it ordered that a proof of service be filed and that a proposed order be provided. Mr. Schmidt stated that he submitted a proposed order yesterday in the Room 202 drop box, that JP Morgan is jerking his Client around regarding the records, and that he is thinking of doing an 8870 petition and haul JP Morgan into Court, but he is hoping that the Court give him authority to subpoena the records. Mr. Schmidt also that they have no power right now to issue a subpoena because this is not a contested matter, that JP Morgan keeps putting up road blocks, that his Client goes to the office and is told to go to corporate, that corporate tells her that she needs a notarized letter and that she receives three months of statements, and then has to provide another notarized letter. In addition, Mr. Schmidt stated that his Client was appointed in 2019 and that they need to account from the date of death. The Court raised the issue of accounting with what they have or getting a waiver of account. Mr. Schmidt stated that he can pursue both tracks. Ms. Hoffman stated that her Client will not waive an accounting, that if the accounting is incomplete, they will have to object and that she prefers Mr. Schmidt issue a subpoena. The Court noted that this has been around since 2017 and stated that they need to get the accounting done and get this estate closed. The Petition for Approval of Report on Status of Administration is under submission. Status on close of estate is set for May 5, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.
